name: Universal 3D Printed Phone Stand - Multi-Design Collection
description: Premium 3D-printed phone stands in multiple iconic designs (bird, minimal base, curved ergonomic) and vibrant color options. Perfect for desks, showcasing phones, tablets, or small devices. 3D-printable files optimized for FDM/SLA printing with elegant proportions, branded cutouts, and stable weighted bases. Sells through Etsy, Thingiverse, and print-on-demand platforms.

visual_analysis.silhouette: Collection of stand designs: (1) Curved organic bird silhouette with rounded back and weighted base, (2) Minimal rectangular base with angled phone slot, (3) Geometric 'A'-frame or chevron design with deep contact pads and cable management, (4) Branded versions with Android/Apple logo cutouts at ~45° angle cradle
key_features: Ergonomic curved support arm at optimal 45-50° angle
Weighted base prevents tipping (integrated mass distribution)
Branded logo cutouts (Android, Apple) for aesthetic appeal
Dual contact pads (rubber/soft-touch compatible) grip phone securely
Cable management slot or routing groove on rear/side
Mounting hole for wall bracket attachment
Minimal overhang reduces support material during printing
Smooth rounded edges reduce phone scratching risk

blender_steps.description: Create base rectangular foundation (65x55mm footprint, 12mm tall). This will be the weighted anchor.
Scale cube to approximate base dimensions: 65mm wide, 55mm deep, 12mm height
Apply smooth shading to base for clean appearance
Create angled support arm/back (main phone-holding structure). Rotated ~45° for ergonomic angle
Shape support arm: 35mm wide, 8mm thick, 95mm height total. Angled orientation for 45° phone cradle
Smooth the support arm surfaces
Create left contact pad (silicone-gripping surface for phone back)
Size contact pad: ~18mm wide, 12mm deep, 32mm tall. Positioned on left side of cradle
Create right contact pad (mirror of left for dual-grip stability)
Position right pad on opposite side, 18mm from center

params.code:
import bpy
from mathutils import Vector

# Create slot/channel for cable management on rear
bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cube_add(size=1.0, location=(0, -0.8, 0.4))
slot = bpy.context.active_object
slot.scale = (0.8, 0.1, 0.5)

# Boolean difference to carve channel
bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = bpy.data.objects.get('Cube')
bpy.ops.object.modifier_add(type='BOOLEAN')
bpy.context.object.modifiers[-1].operation = 'DIFFERENCE'
bpy.context.object.modifiers[-1].object = slot

blender_steps.description: Add cable management slot/channel carved into rear base using boolean operation
Create mounting hole for wall bracket (6mm diameter, 10mm depth)

params.code:
import bpy

# Boolean difference for mounting hole
bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = bpy.data.objects.get('Cylinder')
hole = bpy.context.active_object
bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = bpy.data.objects.get('Cube')
bpy.ops.object.modifier_add(type='BOOLEAN')
bpy.context.object.modifiers[-1].operation = 'DIFFERENCE'
bpy.context.object.modifiers[-1].object = hole

blender_steps.description: Cut mounting hole through base via boolean difference
Create branded logo cutout area (Android/Apple logo recess) - geometric placeholder
Shape logo cutout as shallow depression (2mm depth) across support face

params.code:
import bpy

# Boolean difference for logo recess
bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = bpy.data.objects.get('Cube.004')

bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = bpy.data.objects.get('Cube.001')
bpy.ops.object.modifier_add(type='BOOLEAN')

blender_steps.description: Carve logo cutout depression into support arm
Apply Catmull-Clark subdivision to base for smooth, rounded edges
Apply subdivision to support arm for organic curve
Light subdivision on left contact pad
Light subdivision on right contact pad
Apply vibrant orange (#FF6B35) base material with matte finish (roughness for 3D-print texture)
Apply matching orange to support arm
Apply dark gray to left contact pad (softer appearance for grip surface)
Apply dark gray to right contact pad
Recalculate all normals for watertight mesh export

params.code:
import bpy

# Select all objects and join into single mesh
bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT')
bpy.ops.object.join()
stand_model = bpy.context.active_object
stand_model.name = 'PhoneStand_Orange'

# Apply all modifiers
for mod in stand_model.modifiers:
    bpy.ops.object.modifier_apply(modifier=mod.name)

blender_steps.description: Join all components into single watertight object and apply all modifiers
Remove duplicate vertices for clean geometry

params.code:
import bpy
from mathutils import Vector

# Verify watertight by checking mesh integrity
mesh = bpy.context.active_object.data
print(f'Vertices: {len(mesh.vertices)}')
print(f'Edges: {len(mesh.edges)}')
print(f'Faces: {len(mesh.faces)}')

# Add 1.5mm wall thickness to ensure printability
bpy.ops.object.modifier_add(type='SOLIDIFY')
solidify = bpy.context.object.modifiers[-1]
solidify.thickness = 0.003  # 1.5mm in Blender units (assuming 1 unit = 1mm)

blender_steps.description: Add solidify modifier for minimum 1.5mm wall thickness compliance with 3D-print specifications
Auto-unwrap UV map for efficient texture mapping and print pattern visibility
Export final model as STL (watertight mesh format) optimized for FDM/SLA 3D printing

params.code:
import bpy

# Duplicate model for alternative color variant (Teal/Turquoise)
bpy.ops.object.duplicate(use_linked=False)
teal_stand = bpy.context.active_object
teal_stand.name = 'PhoneStand_Teal'

# Change material color to teal
for mat_slot in teal_stand.material_slots:
    if mat_slot.material:
        mat_slot.material.use_nodes = True
        bsdf = mat_slot.material.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']
        bsdf.inputs[0].default_value = (0.08, 0.72, 0.72, 1.0)  # Teal #14B8A6

blender_steps.description: Duplicate model and create teal color variant (#14B8A6) for product variety
Export teal variant as STL

params.code:
import bpy

# Create white minimalist variant
bpy.ops.object.duplicate(use_linked=False)
white_stand = bpy.context.active_object
white_stand.name = 'PhoneStand_White'

for mat_slot in white_stand.material_slots:
    if mat_slot.material:
        mat_slot.material.use_nodes = True
        bsdf = mat_slot.material.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']
        bsdf.inputs[0].default_value = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0)  # Pure white

blender_steps.description: Create white minimalist variant for neutral/professional aesthetic
Export white variant as STL

params.code:
import bpy

# Get all stand objects and calculate combined triangle count
total_tris = 0
for obj in bpy.data.objects:
    if 'PhoneStand' in obj.name and obj.type == 'MESH':
        tris = len(obj.data.polygons)
        total_tris += tris
        print(f'{obj.name}: {tris} triangles')

print(f'\nTotal triangle count (single model): ~{total_tris}')
print(f'Within budget: {total_tris < 100000}')

blender_steps.description: Verify triangle count is within 100k max budget for platform constraint compliance
